name: Oxford Bank
description_short: Oxford Bank is a Michigan-based FDIC-insured community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and mortgages with digital banking and local branch access.
description_long: Oxford Bank is a community bank with 140 years of history, operating as an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank is powered by First Branch and maintains physical branch locations alongside digital banking capabilities. Their mission emphasizes being a local bank that cares about community involvement and customer relationships. The bank offers comprehensive personal banking products including Kasasa checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, and IRAs. For borrowing, they provide personal loans, home mortgages, home equity lines of credit, auto loans, and student loans. Business customers can access multiple checking and savings account tiers, business term loans, business lines of credit, SBA loans, and commercial real estate loans. Additional services include credit cards through Oxford Commercial Finance, bill pay, mobile wallet, debit cards, and merchant card services for business customers. Oxford Bank distinguishes itself through free financial education programs (Kasasa rewards, GreenPath Financial Wellness, financial literacy courses), community engagement (shred days, client appreciation events), and accessibility features on their website. They offer both personal and business wealth management services and provide accessible online banking enrollment. The bank explicitly warns customers about scam attempts and maintains fraud prevention resources. As a traditional community bank, Oxford Bank is best suited for customers seeking personalized local banking with FDIC protection and comprehensive lending options. However, the website does not disclose specific APRs, fees beyond a fee schedule reference, interest rates, or loan terms, limiting ability to compare competitiveness. Their digital offerings appear functional but not positioned as fintech-driven alternatives to larger national banks.
services: Free Kasasa Cash Checking with cash rewards
Free Kasasa Saver with automated savings features
Personal savings accounts, money market accounts, and CDs
Personal loans, auto loans, and student loans
Home mortgage loans and home equity lines of credit (HELOC)
Business checking and savings accounts with multiple tiers
SBA loans, business term loans, and business lines of credit
Mobile banking and online bill pay (iPay)
Merchant card services and treasury management
Credit cards through Oxford Commercial Finance
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full government backing
Kasasa rewards checking that pays cash back with no monthly fees
Free financial education and wellness programs including GreenPath counseling
Comprehensive product range: checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, personal loans, business loans, and SBA lending
Mobile banking and digital access including online account opening
Community-focused services like free document shredding events
140-year operating history suggesting stability and local market knowledge
cons: Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APRs, or loan terms for comparison
No pricing transparency on fees beyond a referenced fee schedule not shown in content
Limited information about minimum balances or account requirements
Digital banking positioned as supplement to branches rather than primary offering, suggesting weaker fintech capabilities than online-first banks
No mention of customer service hours or response times for support
best_for: Michigan residents seeking FDIC-insured personal and business banking with local branch access
Small business owners needing relationship-based lending and SBA loan options
Customers prioritizing community bank values and personalized service over digital-only platforms
Families looking for free financial education and wealth management alongside deposit products
diagnosis: Oxford Bank is best for Michigan customers and small business owners seeking FDIC-insured banking with local branch relationships, community engagement, and comprehensive lending options. The main caveat is lack of rate and fee transparency on the website—prospective customers must contact the bank directly to compare competitiveness against other banks and online alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Oxford Bank is a Michigan community bank offering checking, savings, loans, and mortgages.
